2603.23114.title: Between Rules and Reality: On the Context Sensitivity of LLM Moral Judgment
2603.23114.paper_summary: A human's moral decision depends heavily on the context. Yet research on LLM morality has largely studied fixed scenarios. We address this gap by introducing Contextual MoralChoice, a dataset of moral dilemmas with systematic contextual variations known from moral psychology to shift human judgment: consequentialist, emotional, and relational. Evaluating 22 LLMs, we find that nearly all models are context-sensitive, shifting their judgments toward rule-violating behavior. Comparing with a human survey, we find that models and humans are most triggered by different contextual variations, and that a model aligned with human judgments in the base case is not necessarily aligned in its contextual sensitivity. This raises the question of controlling contextual sensitivity, which we address with an activation steering approach that can reliably increase or decrease a model's contextual sensitivity.
2603.23114.paper_summary_zh: 人類的道德決策在很大程度上依賴於情境。<lb/>然而，對於大型語言模型（LLM）道德的研究主要集中在固定情境上。<lb/>我們通過引入Contextual MoralChoice，這是一個具有系統性情境變化的道德困境數據集，來填補這一空白，這些變化在道德心理學中被認為會影響人類判斷：後果主義、情感和關係。<lb/>在評估22個LLM時，我們發現幾乎所有模型都是情境敏感的，並且其判斷傾向於違反規則的行為。<lb/>與人類調查相比，我們發現模型和人類對不同的情境變化的反應最為明顯，並且在基本情境中與人類判斷一致的模型不一定在其情境敏感性上保持一致。<lb/>這引發了控制情境敏感性的問題，我們通過一種激活引導的方法來解決這一問題，該方法可以可靠地增加或減少模型的情境敏感性。
2603.23114.author: Adrian Sauter et.al.
